title: abronia umbellata var. minor
intro: Abronia umbellata var. minor, also known as lesser orlaya, is a plant species that belongs to the Abronia genus, characterized by its small, white or pale yellow flowers and narrow, thread-like leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Abronia umbellata var. minor include relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and infusing the leaves and flowers, and can be taken as teas or tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties.
